name: Split Seconds
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a large bowl, beat together butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add vanilla and egg; blend well.
4. Stir in flour and baking powder; mix thoroughly.
5. Divide the dough into four equal portions.
6. On a lightly floured surface, shape each portion into 12 x 3/4-inch rolls.
7. Place the rolls on ungreased cookie sheets.
8. Using the handle of a wooden spoon or your finger, make a depression about 1/2 inch wide and 1/4 inch deep along the center of each roll.
9. Fill each roll with approximately 2 tablespoons of jelly.
10. Bake at 350°F for 15 to 20 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
11. Allow cookies to cool slightly, then cut diagonally into strips.
12. Let cool completely on wire racks.
Nutrition per cookie: Calories 70; Protein 1g; Carbohydrates 9g; Fat 3g; Sodium 40mg
